_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience/Fellowship in Hematology/Medical Oncology)
clinical_indications: CBC, Peripheral Smear, Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy (may not be done at all centres), FISH on bone marrow or RT-PCR on peripheral blood or bone marrow, LFT, RFT required for diagnosis
Dose as per the phase of disease
Dose may need to be increased based on the response – documentation of time from the start of imatinib and response date, response category (match as per the NCG guidelines)
Prior therapy if any, details with response

preauth.label: Clinical notes (including prior treatment, response to therapy, spleen size on clinical examination)
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy (may not be done at all centres)
Conventional cytogenetics or FISH on bone marrow or RT-PCR on peripheral blood or bone marrow
Documentation of time from the start of imatinib and response date, response category (if dose increase is requested)

claim.label: Reports of all tests (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.) listed for pre-authorization

ppd_preauth.q: Is the patient on palliative care?
Does the proposed Imatinib dose (CP-400 mg, AP/BC-600/800 mg) match the documented phase of disease?

cpd_claim.q: Has response monitoring (e.g., BCR-ABL) been done within the last year?
Has CBC been done within the last 6 months?
Is BCR-ABL increasing over the last 3 visits?
In the continuation phase, are TLC rising or platelets below 50,000/cumm without justification?

common_queries: Patient indicated as being on palliative care, which is a contraindication.
The prescribed Imatinib dose does not match the documented phase of disease.
Response monitoring (e.g., BCR-ABL) has not been performed for over one year.
CBC report not available for the last 6 months.
BCR-ABL level is increasing over the last 3 visits without clinical justification.
In the continuation phase, TLC is rising or platelets are below 50,000/cumm without clinical justification.
Incomplete mandatory documents submitted at pre-authorization.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Qualified Medical Oncologist/Hematologist; OPD basis treatment/ May need transfusion in Daycare
clinical_key_pointers: The National Cancer Grid (NCG) has issued clinical guidelines for Chronic Myeloid Leukemia to be followed in our country.
This package is given once in lifetime.
Mandatory parameters to be monitored: CBC, LFT, and RFT.
Initial therapy: BM FISH and/or cytogenetics at 6 months from the start of Imatinib (if positive, to be repeated at 1 year). The alternative is RQ-PCR on peripheral blood at 6, 12 months.
Continuation therapy: RQ-PCR on peripheral blood once every 6-12 months.
Repeat Bone marrow aspiration, cytogenetics and biopsy may be done if RQ-PCR or peripheral smear suggests disease progression.

Follow-up care: OPD visits at least once in 3 months; CBC, LFT, RFT, and follow-up tests as listed above (Initial therapy - BM FISH and/or cytogenetics at 6 months from the start of Imatinib if positive, to be repeated at 1 year. The alternative is RQ-PCR on peripheral blood at 6, 12 months).
